goal: Agent streaming UI — live tool activity in game panels, header ticker, and improved sprite status text

risks: 1. Header ticker animation complexity — cycling through agents with smooth transitions could become janky if not carefully throttled, especially with many concurrent active agents. Risk of layout shift in the header.

2. Sprite status text rendering in Canvas 2D is constrained by the bitmap font system — showing tool calls with file paths requires careful truncation and may look unreadable at normal zoom levels. The 20-char limit exists for a reason.

3. Streaming panel in AgentPanel could cause excessive re-renders if sessionActivities updates are high-frequency. Need to ensure React rendering is throttled or memoized to avoid perf issues in the side panel.
over_engineering_flags: The directive is well-scoped — all three requirements share the same data pipeline (sessionActivities from WebSocket) and are purely frontend rendering. No over-engineering risk.
challenges.recommendation: Proceed as-is. All three requirements are tightly coupled (same data source, same UI layer, same domain). Single project is the correct decomposition.

projects.title: Agent streaming UI: session panel, header ticker, sprite status
projects.scope_summary: Three connected frontend changes all driven by the existing sessionActivities WebSocket data. (1) Enhance AgentPanel to show a streaming log of tool calls with file paths, auto-scrolling, styled like a CLI output. (2) Add a rolling status ticker to GameHeader center area that cycles through active agents showing what they're doing. (3) Fix sprite status text in renderer.ts — replace the dim white 0.35-alpha text with better-styled, more useful streaming status using the agent's brand color.
